# Recipe Collection App

## Overview
A personal recipe management application where users can save, organize, and search through their favorite recipes. Designed for individual use with a focus on quick recipe retrieval and easy organization.

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend**: React with TypeScript
- **Backend**: Node.js with Express
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS
- **Authentication**: JWT tokens
- **File Storage**: Local filesystem (with option to upgrade to cloud storage)
- **Search**: PostgreSQL full-text search with pg_trgm extension

## Data Model

### User
- id: UUID (primary key)
- email: string (unique, required) — login identifier
- password_hash: string (required) — bcrypt hashed password
- name: string (required) — display name
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp

### Recipe
- id: UUID (primary key)
- user_id: UUID (foreign key to User) — recipe owner
- title: string (required, max 200 chars) — recipe name
- description: text (optional) — brief recipe summary
- ingredients: text (required) — ingredient list, stored as formatted text
- instructions: text (required) — cooking steps
- prep_time: integer (optional) — minutes
- cook_time: integer (optional) — minutes
- servings: integer (optional) — number of servings
- difficulty: enum ['easy', 'medium', 'hard'] (optional)
- cuisine_type: string (optional, max 50 chars) — e.g., "Italian", "Mexican"
- image_url: string (optional) — path to recipe photo
- tags: string[] (optional) — searchable keywords
- is_favorite: boolean (default false) — quick favorite toggle
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User

## User Flows

### Registration & Login
1. User visits app → sees login/register form
2. New user clicks "Sign Up" → enters email, name, password → system validates (email format, password min 8 chars) → on success: creates account and redirects to recipe list / on failure: shows inline errors
3. Returning user enters email/password → system validates → on success: sets JWT token and redirects to recipe list / on failure: shows "Invalid credentials" error
4. Forgot password → user enters email → system sends reset link → user clicks link → enters new password → redirects to login

### Recipe Management
1. User clicks "Add Recipe" → sees recipe form with fields: title, description, ingredients (textarea), instructions (textarea), prep time, cook time, servings, difficulty dropdown, cuisine type, tags (comma-separated), photo upload
2. User fills form → clicks "Save Recipe" → system validates required fields → on success: saves recipe and redirects to recipe detail page with success toast / on failure: shows field-specific errors
3. User views recipe list → clicks recipe title → sees full recipe detail page with edit/delete buttons
4. User clicks "Edit" → sees pre-filled form → makes changes → saves → returns to detail page with "Recipe updated" message
5. User clicks "Delete" → sees confirmation modal "Are you sure? This cannot be undone." → confirms → recipe deleted with "Recipe deleted" toast → redirects to recipe list

### Search & Browse
1. User sees recipe list with search bar at top and filter options (cuisine type, difficulty, favorites only)
2. User types in search → system searches title, ingredients, instructions, and tags in real-time (debounced 300ms) → results update below
3. User applies filters → results update to match criteria
4. User clicks "Favorites" toggle → shows only recipes marked as favorite
5. Empty search results show "No recipes found. Try different keywords or check your filters."
6. User clicks heart icon on any recipe → toggles favorite status with immediate visual feedback

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **User Authentication System**
   - Registration form with email/name/password fields
   - Login form with email/password
   - JWT token generation and validation middleware
   - Password hashing with bcrypt (min 8 characters, must contain letter and number)
   - Protected routes that redirect to login if not authenticated
   - Logout functionality that clears token
   - Basic password reset via email (send reset link, validate token, update password)

2. **Database Setup**
   - PostgreSQL database with User and Recipe tables
   - Prisma schema and migrations
   - Database indexes on user_id, title, and created_at for performance
   - Full-text search indexes on title, ingredients, instructions, and tags

### Phase 2: Core Recipe Management
3. **Recipe CRUD Operations**
   - Create recipe form with all fields, client-side validation
   - Recipe list page showing title, cuisine, prep time, favorite status in card layout
   - Recipe detail page with full recipe display and edit/delete buttons
   - Edit recipe (pre-populate form, update functionality)
   - Delete recipe with confirmation modal
   - Image upload for recipe photos (validate file type: jpg, png, webp; max 5MB)

4. **Recipe List & Display**
   - Paginated recipe list (20 recipes per page)
   - Recipe cards showing: photo thumbnail, title, cuisine type, prep/cook time, favorite heart icon
   - Responsive grid layout (1 column mobile, 2 columns tablet, 3 columns desktop)
   - Empty state when user has no recipes: "No recipes yet. Add your first recipe to get started!"
   - Loading skeleton while recipes are fetching

### Phase 3: Search & Organization
5. **Search Functionality**
   - Search bar with real-time results (debounced 300ms)
   - Full-text search across title, ingredients, instructions, and tags
   - Search results highlighting matching terms
   - Search persistence in URL query params
   - "Clear search" button when search is active

6. **Filtering & Favorites**
   - Filter dropdown for cuisine type (populated from existing recipes)
   - Filter dropdown for difficulty level
   - "Favorites only" toggle switch
   - Filter combinations work together (AND logic)
   - Filter state preserved in URL
   - Favorite toggle on recipe cards with immediate visual feedback
   - Favorite toggle on recipe detail page

## Validation Rules
- **Email**: Valid email format, unique across users
- **Password**: Minimum 8 characters, must contain at least one letter and one number
- **Recipe title**: Required, 1-200 characters
- **Ingredients**: Required, minimum 10 characters
- **Instructions**: Required, minimum 20 characters
- **Prep/cook time**: If provided, must be positive integer under 1440 (24 hours)
- **Servings**: If provided, must be positive integer under 100
- **Image upload**: jpg, png, or webp format; maximum 5MB file size
- **Tags**: If provided, each tag max 30 characters, max 10 tags per recipe

## Error Handling
- **Form validation errors**: Show inline below each field with red text and border
- **Network errors**: Show toast notification "Something went wrong. Please try again."
- **Authentication errors**: Redirect to login with message "Please log in to continue"
- **File upload errors**: Show specific message ("File too large" or "Invalid file type")
- **Search errors**: Graceful fallback showing cached results with "Search temporarily unavailable"
- **404 errors**: Custom page with "Recipe not found" and link back to recipe list

## UI/UX Notes
- **Layout**: Clean, Pinterest-style card grid for recipe browsing
- **Colors**: Warm, food-friendly palette (consider oranges, greens, warm whites)
- **Typography**: Readable fonts for recipe instructions (consider Georgia or similar serif for body text)
- **Mobile-first**: Touch-friendly buttons (min 44px), easy thumb navigation
- **Accessibility**: Alt text for recipe images, keyboard navigation, focus indicators, proper heading hierarchy
- **Loading states**: Skeleton screens for recipe cards, spinner for form submissions
- **Success feedback**: Green toast notifications for saves/updates/deletes
- **Image handling**: Automatic thumbnail generation, lazy loading for recipe list

## Assumptions
- **Single user per account**: No recipe sharing or collaboration features
- **Local storage**: Recipe images stored on server filesystem (not cloud storage initially)
- **Simple tagging**: Free-form tags entered as comma-separated text (no tag autocomplete)
- **Basic search**: PostgreSQL full-text search (no advanced search operators or filters)
- **No recipe import**: Users manually enter recipes (no import from URLs or other apps)
- **No meal planning**: Pure recipe storage and retrieval (no calendar or planning features)
- **No ratings or reviews**: Personal collection only (no social features)
- **No recipe scaling**: Ingredient quantities not automatically adjusted for different serving sizes
- **Email for password reset**: Assumes SMTP server available for sending reset emails

## Out of Scope
- Recipe sharing with other users
- Social features (comments, ratings, following other users)
- Meal planning or calendar integration
- Grocery list generation from recipes
- Recipe import from websites or other apps
- Advanced search with boolean operators
- Recipe categorization beyond basic tags
- Nutritional information calculation
- Recipe scaling/portion adjustment
- Print-optimized recipe layouts
- Mobile app (web-only initially)

## Self-Assessment

**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 4/5
- Specificity: 4/5  
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 4/5
- Translation Quality: 4/5

**Issues caught and fixed:**
- Added specific validation rules for all form fields (originally just mentioned "validation")
- Specified exact UI patterns for search (debounced real-time) and favorites (heart icon toggle)
- Added comprehensive error handling for network issues, file uploads, and edge cases
- Included loading states and empty states that weren't in the original request
- Added accessibility considerations and mobile-responsive behavior
- Specified image upload constraints and handling that the user didn't mention
- Added URL persistence for search and filters to maintain state on page refresh

**Remaining considerations:**
- Image storage strategy could be upgraded to cloud storage (S3, Cloudinary) for better performance
- Search could be enhanced with Elasticsearch for more advanced features
- Consider adding recipe categories/folders if the user accumulates many recipes

## Questions for Consideration
1. Would you like recipe import functionality (paste a URL and auto-extract recipe data)?
2. Do you want nutritional information calculated automatically from ingredients?
3. Should recipes be printable with a print-friendly layout?
4. Would you like to organize recipes into folders/categories beyond just tags?
5. Do you need recipe scaling (automatically adjust ingredient quantities for different serving sizes)?

# Handmade Crafts Marketplace

## Overview
A two-sided marketplace platform where artisans can list and sell handmade crafts, buyers can browse and purchase items, and both parties can engage through reviews and ratings. Think Etsy-style marketplace with integrated payments and review system.

## Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 14 with TypeScript (full-stack React with API routes)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Authentication**: NextAuth.js with email/password and Google OAuth
- **Payments**: Stripe Connect (enables marketplace payments with seller payouts)
- **File Storage**: AWS S3 for product images
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS
- **Email**: Resend for transactional emails
- **Deployment**: Vercel (seamless Next.js integration)

## Data Model

### User
- id: UUID (primary key)
- email: string (unique, required) — login identifier
- password_hash: string (nullable) — null for OAuth-only users
- first_name: string (required) — display name component
- last_name: string (required) — display name component
- role: enum ['buyer', 'seller', 'both'] (default: 'buyer') — determines available features
- stripe_customer_id: string (nullable) — for buyer payments
- stripe_account_id: string (nullable) — for seller payouts (Stripe Connect)
- profile_image_url: string (nullable) — avatar
- bio: text (nullable) — seller description
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- email_verified: boolean (default: false)
- Relationships: has_many Products, has_many Orders (as buyer), has_many Orders (as seller through products), has_many Reviews (as author), has_many Reviews (as recipient through products)

### Product
- id: UUID (primary key)
- seller_id: UUID (foreign key to User) — who's selling this
- title: string (required, max 100 chars) — product name
- description: text (required, max 2000 chars) — detailed description
- price: decimal(10,2) (required, min 0.01) — price in USD
- category: enum ['jewelry', 'clothing', 'home_decor', 'art', 'toys', 'accessories', 'other'] — browsing organization
- status: enum ['draft', 'active', 'sold', 'archived'] (default: 'draft') — availability
- quantity: integer (default: 1, min 0) — stock level
- weight_oz: decimal(5,2) (nullable) — for shipping calculations
- processing_time_days: integer (default: 3, min 1, max 30) — how long to make/ship
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User (seller), has_many ProductImages, has_many OrderItems, has_many Reviews

### ProductImage
- id: UUID (primary key)
- product_id: UUID (foreign key to Product)
- image_url: string (required) — S3 URL
- alt_text: string (required) — accessibility description
- sort_order: integer (default: 0) — display order
- is_primary: boolean (default: false) — main product photo
- Relationships: belongs_to Product

### Order
- id: UUID (primary key)
- buyer_id: UUID (foreign key to User) — who's buying
- status: enum ['pending', 'paid', 'processing', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled'] (default: 'pending')
- subtotal: decimal(10,2) (required) — sum of line items
- tax_amount: decimal(10,2) (default: 0) — calculated tax
- shipping_amount: decimal(10,2) (default: 0) — shipping cost
- total_amount: decimal(10,2) (required) — final amount charged
- stripe_payment_intent_id: string (nullable) — Stripe reference
- shipping_address_line1: string (required)
- shipping_address_line2: string (nullable)
- shipping_city: string (required)
- shipping_state: string (required)
- shipping_zip: string (required)
- shipping_country: string (default: 'US')
- tracking_number: string (nullable) — provided by seller
- shipped_at: timestamp (nullable)
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User (buyer), has_many OrderItems

### OrderItem
- id: UUID (primary key)
- order_id: UUID (foreign key to Order)
- product_id: UUID (foreign key to Product)
- quantity: integer (required, min 1) — how many of this product
- unit_price: decimal(10,2) (required) — price at time of purchase (frozen)
- total_price: decimal(10,2) (required) — quantity × unit_price
- Relationships: belongs_to Order, belongs_to Product

### Review
- id: UUID (primary key)
- product_id: UUID (foreign key to Product) — what's being reviewed
- author_id: UUID (foreign key to User) — who wrote the review
- order_item_id: UUID (foreign key to OrderItem) — proves purchase (required for review)
- rating: integer (required, min 1, max 5) — star rating
- title: string (nullable, max 100 chars) — review headline
- content: text (nullable, max 1000 chars) — review body
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to Product, belongs_to User (author), belongs_to OrderItem

## User Flows

### Buyer Registration & Setup
1. User visits homepage → clicks "Sign Up" → sees registration form
2. User enters email, password, first name, last name → submits → system validates (email format, password strength 8+ chars, required fields)
3. On success: account created, verification email sent → user sees "Check your email" page
4. User clicks verification link → email_verified set to true → redirects to marketplace browse page
5. On failure: form shows inline errors, preserves valid input

### Seller Onboarding
1. Existing user (or during registration) → clicks "Start Selling" → sees seller setup form
2. User fills bio, uploads profile image → submits → role updated to 'seller' or 'both'
3. System initiates Stripe Connect onboarding → user completes Stripe account setup
4. On Stripe completion: stripe_account_id saved → user can now list products

### Product Listing Creation
1. Seller navigates to "My Products" → clicks "Add New Product" → sees product form
2. Seller fills title, description, price, category, quantity, processing time → uploads 1-5 images (first is primary)
3. Form validates: title length, price > 0, at least 1 image, required fields
4. Seller clicks "Save as Draft" → product saved with status='draft' → redirects to product preview
5. From preview, seller clicks "Publish" → status changed to 'active' → product appears in marketplace

### Product Browsing & Search
1. Buyer visits marketplace → sees grid of active products with primary images, titles, prices
2. Buyer can filter by category (dropdown) and search by title/description (search bar with submit button)
3. Clicking a product → navigates to product detail page showing all images, full description, seller info, reviews
4. Product page shows "Add to Cart" button (if quantity > 0) or "Out of Stock" message

### Purchase Flow
1. From product page, buyer clicks "Add to Cart" → item added, cart icon shows count
2. Buyer clicks cart → sees cart page with items, quantities (editable), subtotal
3. Buyer clicks "Checkout" → redirects to checkout form (shipping address, payment method)
4. Buyer fills shipping info → clicks "Place Order" → Stripe payment processing begins
5. On payment success: order created with status='paid', inventory decremented, confirmation email sent → redirects to order confirmation page
6. On payment failure: user stays on checkout with error message, can retry

### Order Fulfillment
1. Seller receives email notification of new order → logs in to see "Orders to Ship" dashboard
2. Seller clicks order → sees order details, shipping address, buyer info
3. Seller prepares item → clicks "Mark as Processing" → order status updated, buyer notified
4. When shipped, seller enters tracking number → clicks "Mark as Shipped" → order status updated, buyer gets tracking info

### Review System
1. After order status becomes 'delivered' (or 7 days after 'shipped'), buyer receives "Leave a Review" email
2. Buyer clicks review link → sees review form for each product in that order
3. Buyer selects star rating (required), optionally adds title and written review → submits
4. Review appears on product page immediately, seller gets notification email
5. Reviews display with buyer name, rating, date, and content on product pages

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **User Authentication System**
   - Registration form with email/password and Google OAuth option
   - Email verification flow with token-based confirmation
   - Login/logout with session management
   - Password reset flow with email tokens
   - User profile pages showing basic info and seller bio (if applicable)
   - Acceptance criteria: Users can register, verify email, log in/out, reset password, view/edit profiles
   - Edge cases: Handle duplicate emails, expired tokens, OAuth account linking
   - UI: Clean forms with inline validation, loading states on submit, success/error messages

2. **Basic Product Management**
   - Product CRUD for sellers (create, read, update, delete)
   - Image upload with drag-and-drop interface, preview before submit
   - Product status management (draft → active → archived)
   - Basic product listing page showing seller's products
   - Acceptance criteria: Sellers can create products with images, save as draft, publish, edit, archive
   - Edge cases: Handle large images (resize), invalid file types, missing required fields
   - UI: Multi-step form for product creation, image gallery with reorder capability, status indicators

### Phase 2: Core Marketplace
3. **Product Browsing & Search**
   - Public marketplace page with product grid (paginated, 20 per page)
   - Category filtering with dropdown, search by title/description
   - Product detail pages with image carousel, full description, seller info
   - Responsive grid layout (4 columns desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile)
   - Acceptance criteria: Buyers can browse all active products, filter by category, search, view details
   - Edge cases: Empty search results, no products in category, broken images
   - UI: Card-based product grid, breadcrumb navigation, skeleton loading states

4. **Shopping Cart & Checkout**
   - Add to cart functionality with quantity selection
   - Cart page with item management (update quantities, remove items)
   - Checkout form with shipping address and Stripe payment integration
   - Order confirmation page with order details and tracking info
   - Acceptance criteria: Buyers can add items to cart, modify cart, complete purchase with card payment
   - Edge cases: Out of stock items, payment failures, address validation, cart persistence across sessions
   - UI: Floating cart icon with count, checkout progress indicator, payment form with Stripe Elements

### Phase 3: Order Management
5. **Order Processing System**
   - Order dashboard for buyers (order history, status tracking)
   - Order management for sellers (new orders, shipping workflow)
   - Email notifications for order status changes
   - Inventory management (decrement on purchase, prevent overselling)
   - Acceptance criteria: Orders flow from paid → processing → shipped → delivered with notifications
   - Edge cases: Concurrent purchases of last item, failed email delivery, order cancellations
   - UI: Status timeline, order detail modals, bulk actions for sellers

6. **Stripe Connect Integration**
   - Seller onboarding flow for Stripe Connect accounts
   - Marketplace payment processing (platform fee handling)
   - Seller payout management and reporting
   - Payment failure handling and retry logic
   - Acceptance criteria: Sellers receive payouts minus platform fees, payment disputes are handled
   - Edge cases: Stripe account verification failures, payout delays, refund processing
   - UI: Onboarding wizard, earnings dashboard, payout history

### Phase 4: Reviews & Polish
7. **Review System**
   - Review form accessible only to verified purchasers
   - Review display on product pages with ratings aggregation
   - Review moderation (flag inappropriate content)
   - Seller response capability to reviews
   - Acceptance criteria: Only buyers who purchased can review, reviews show on products, ratings aggregate correctly
   - Edge cases: Multiple reviews per buyer per product (prevent), deleted products with reviews, spam detection
   - UI: Star rating input, review cards with helpful/unhelpful voting, moderation flags

8. **Enhanced Features**
   - Seller analytics dashboard (sales, views, conversion rates)
   - Wishlist/favorites for buyers
   - Product recommendations based on browsing history
   - Advanced search with price ranges and sorting options
   - Acceptance criteria: Sellers can track performance, buyers can save favorites, search is comprehensive
   - Edge cases: Analytics with no data, recommendation cold start, complex search queries
   - UI: Charts and graphs for analytics, heart icons for favorites, advanced search filters

## Validation Rules

| Field | Rules | Error Messages |
|-------|-------|----------------|
| User.email | Valid email format, unique | "Please enter a valid email address", "Email already registered" |
| User.password | Min 8 characters, at least 1 number | "Password must be at least 8 characters with 1 number" |
| User.first_name, last_name | Required, max 50 chars | "Name is required", "Name too long" |
| Product.title | Required, max 100 chars | "Title is required", "Title must be under 100 characters" |
| Product.description | Required, max 2000 chars | "Description is required", "Description too long" |
| Product.price | Required, min $0.01, max $10,000 | "Price is required", "Minimum price is $0.01", "Maximum price is $10,000" |
| Product.quantity | Integer, min 0 | "Quantity must be 0 or greater" |
| ProductImage | Max 5 images per product, max 5MB each, JPG/PNG only | "Maximum 5 images allowed", "Image must be under 5MB", "Only JPG and PNG files allowed" |
| Review.rating | Required, integer 1-5 | "Rating is required", "Rating must be 1-5 stars" |
| Review.content | Max 1000 chars | "Review must be under 1000 characters" |
| Order shipping fields | Required for all address fields except line2 | "Shipping address is required" |

## Error Handling

**Form Submission Errors:**
- Display validation errors inline below each field with red text and error icon
- Show general form errors at the top in a red alert box
- Preserve user input on validation failure (don't clear the form)
- Disable submit button during processing, show loading spinner

**Payment Errors:**
- Stripe errors displayed above payment form with specific messaging
- Network errors show "Payment failed, please try again" with retry button
- Failed payments don't create orders, inventory isn't decremented

**File Upload Errors:**
- Show progress bar during upload, error message if upload fails
- Validate file type/size client-side before upload attempt
- Fallback to default placeholder image if image fails to load

**API/Network Errors:**
- Show toast notifications for background failures (email sending, etc.)
- Graceful degradation: if search fails, show cached results with warning
- Retry mechanisms for critical operations (payment processing, order creation)

**404/Access Errors:**
- Custom 404 page with navigation back to marketplace
- "Access denied" page for unauthorized actions (editing others' products)
- Redirect to login for authentication-required pages

## UI/UX Notes

**Layout & Navigation:**
- Fixed header with logo, search bar, cart icon, user menu
- Responsive breakpoints: 768px (tablet), 1024px (desktop)
- Sidebar navigation for seller dashboard, collapsible on mobile
- Breadcrumb navigation on product and category pages

**Interactive Elements:**
- All buttons have hover states (darker background) and loading states (spinner + disabled)
- Form inputs have focus rings and validation states (green checkmark, red error)
- Product cards have subtle hover elevation and image zoom on hover
- Destructive actions (delete product, cancel order) require confirmation modals

**Loading & Empty States:**
- Skeleton screens for product grids while loading
- Empty state illustrations for no products, no orders, empty cart
- Loading spinners for form submissions, image uploads, search results
- Optimistic UI for cart additions (immediate count update, rollback on failure)

**Mobile Optimization:**
- Touch-friendly button sizes (min 44px height)
- Swipeable image carousels on product pages
- Collapsible filters on mobile search
- Sticky "Add to Cart" button on mobile product pages

**Accessibility:**
- All images have descriptive alt text
- Form labels properly associated with inputs
- Keyboard navigation for all interactive elements
- Focus management in modals (trap focus, return to trigger on close)
- Color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards
- Screen reader announcements for dynamic content changes

## Assumptions

- **Geographic scope**: US-only initially (single currency, shipping addresses, tax calculation)
- **Platform fees**: 3% transaction fee charged to sellers (configurable)
- **Payment processing**: Stripe only (no PayPal, Apple Pay, etc. in v1)
- **Product categories**: Fixed set of 7 categories (not user-defined or hierarchical)
- **Shipping**: Sellers handle their own shipping (no integrated shipping calculator)
- **Tax handling**: Simple tax calculation based on buyer location (not complex multi-jurisdiction)
- **Content moderation**: Basic profanity filter, manual review for flagged content
- **Multi-language**: English only initially
- **Product variations**: No size/color variants (each variant is a separate product)
- **Inventory tracking**: Simple quantity-based (no reserved/pending stock)
- **Return policy**: Handled between buyer and seller (no platform-mediated returns)
- **Seller verification**: Stripe Connect verification is sufficient (no additional vetting)
- **Image storage**: AWS S3 with CDN for performance (not local storage)
- **Email delivery**: Transactional emails only (no marketing campaigns)
- **Search functionality**: Basic text search (not full-text search engine like Elasticsearch)

## Out of Scope

- Multi-vendor shipping calculator integration
- Advanced product variants (size, color, custom options)
- Subscription/recurring payment products
- Digital product delivery (downloads, licenses)
- Wholesale/bulk pricing tiers
- Advanced seller analytics (conversion funnels, A/B testing)
- Social features (following sellers, product sharing)
- Mobile app (web-responsive only)
- Multi-language/internationalization
- Advanced fraud detection beyond Stripe's built-in protection
- Integrated customer service chat
- Seller performance metrics and ranking algorithms
- Advanced search with faceted filtering, autocomplete
- Product bundling or cross-selling features

## Self-Assessment

**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 5/5
- Specificity: 5/5  
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 4/5

3. **Product complexity**: I assumed simple products without variants (size, color, etc.). Do your sellers need to offer products with multiple options?

4. **Shipping integration**: I assumed sellers handle shipping themselves. Do you want integrated shipping calculators (UPS, FedEx) or is seller-managed shipping sufficient?

5. **Content moderation**: How hands-on do you want to be with reviewing products and reviews? Automated filtering only, or manual approval processes?

I'll help you add a book reservation system to your existing Rails bookstore app. Let me start with a draft specification based on what you've described, then ask some clarifying questions.

## Book Reservation System - Draft Specification

### Overview
Add a reservation system allowing customers to reserve books online and pick them up in-store within a specified timeframe.

### Data Model Changes

#### New Model: Reservation
- customer_id: integer (foreign key to User/Customer) — who made the reservation
- book_id: integer (foreign key to Book) — which book is reserved
- status: string (pending/ready/picked_up/expired/cancelled) — reservation lifecycle
- reserved_at: datetime — when reservation was created
- expires_at: datetime — when reservation expires if not picked up
- picked_up_at: datetime — when customer collected the book
- notes: text (optional) — staff notes about the reservation
- Relationships: belongs_to :customer, belongs_to :book

#### Book Model Updates
- Add method to check available quantity for reservations
- Track reserved vs. available inventory

### User Flows

#### Customer Reservation Flow
1. Customer browses books → sees "Reserve for Pickup" button on available books
2. Customer clicks reserve → confirmation modal shows pickup timeframe and store location
3. Customer confirms → reservation created with "pending" status
4. Customer receives confirmation email with reservation details and pickup instructions
5. Customer can view their reservations in account dashboard

#### Staff Fulfillment Flow
1. Staff views pending reservations dashboard
2. Staff locates book and marks reservation as "ready"
3. Customer receives notification that book is ready for pickup
4. Customer arrives → staff finds reservation → marks as "picked_up"

#### Expiration Flow
1. Daily job checks for expired reservations
2. Expired reservations are marked as "expired"
3. Inventory is released back to available stock

### Features (Implementation Order)

#### Phase 1: Core Reservation System
1. **Reservation Model & Associations**
   - Generate Reservation model with fields above
   - Add associations to existing Customer/User and Book models
   - Add validation: customer can't reserve same book twice while active reservation exists

2. **Reserve Button & Modal**
   - Add "Reserve for Pickup" button to book show/index pages (only show if book available)
   - Confirmation modal with pickup timeframe, store hours, location
   - Form submission creates reservation with "pending" status

3. **Customer Reservation Dashboard**
   - New page showing customer's active and past reservations
   - Status indicators, pickup deadline, book details
   - Cancel button for pending reservations

#### Phase 2: Staff Management
4. **Staff Reservations Dashboard**
   - Admin-only page listing all reservations by status
   - Filter by status, date, customer
   - Quick actions to mark ready/picked up

5. **Inventory Integration**
   - Update book availability logic to account for active reservations
   - Prevent over-reserving beyond available stock

#### Phase 3: Automation & Polish
6. **Email Notifications**
   - Confirmation email on reservation creation
   - Ready-for-pickup notification email
   - Expiration warning email (1 day before)

7. **Expiration Job**
   - Daily background job to expire old reservations
   - Release inventory back to available stock

### Assumptions Made
- You have existing Customer/User and Book models
- Books have an inventory/quantity field to track stock
- You're using ActionMailer for emails
- Staff have admin role/permissions to access reservation management
- Pickup timeframe is 7 days (configurable)
- Single store location (not multi-location)
- Reservations are free (no payment required)
- One copy per reservation (customer can't reserve quantity > 1)

---

## Clarifying Questions

Before I finalize this spec, I need to clarify a few key decisions:

1. **Reservation Duration**: How long should customers have to pick up their reserved books? (3 days? 1 week? 2 weeks?)

2. **Inventory Model**: How do you currently track book inventory? Do you have a `quantity` or `stock_count` field on books, or a separate inventory system?

3. **Customer Model**: What's your existing customer/user setup? Do customers need accounts to make reservations, or should there be a guest reservation option with just name/email/phone?

4. **Multiple Copies**: If you have multiple copies of the same book, should customers be able to reserve more than one copy at a time, or limit it to one per customer?

5. **Staff Workflow**: Do you want staff to manually mark books as "ready for pickup" after pulling them from shelves, or should reservations automatically be "ready" when created?

Once you answer these, I'll produce the final refined specification with all the implementation details.

---

Perfect! That context helps a lot. Let me update the draft with Rails 7/Hotwire patterns and integrate with your existing models.

## Updated Book Reservation System - Draft Specification

### Tech Stack Integration
- Rails 7 with Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus)
- PostgreSQL with existing Book, Customer, Order models
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Devise authentication (customers must be logged in to reserve)

### Data Model Changes

#### New Model: Reservation
```ruby
# app/models/reservation.rb
class Reservation < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :customer
  belongs_to :book
  
  enum status: { pending: 0, ready: 1, picked_up: 2, expired: 3, cancelled: 4 }
  
  validates :customer_id, uniqueness: { scope: :book_id, conditions: -> { where(status: [:pending, :ready]) } }
  validates :expires_at, presence: true
  
  scope :active, -> { where(status: [:pending, :ready]) }
  scope :expiring_soon, -> { where(expires_at: 1.day.from_now..Time.current) }
end
```

#### Book Model Updates
```ruby
# Add to existing Book model
has_many :reservations, dependent: :destroy
has_many :active_reservations, -> { where(status: [:pending, :ready]) }, class_name: 'Reservation'

def available_for_reservation?
  stock_count > active_reservations.count
end

def reserved_count
  active_reservations.count
end
```

#### Customer Model Updates
```ruby
# Add to existing Customer model  
has_many :reservations, dependent: :destroy
has_many :active_reservations, -> { where(status: [:pending, :ready]) }, class_name: 'Reservation'
```

### Hotwire Integration Points
- Turbo Frames for reservation button/status updates
- Turbo Streams for real-time dashboard updates
- Stimulus controllers for reservation modal and confirmation flows

### User Flows

#### Customer Reservation Flow (Hotwire-Enhanced)
1. Customer browses catalog → "Reserve for Pickup" button shows if `book.available_for_reservation?`
2. Click triggers Turbo Frame modal with pickup details and confirmation
3. Form submission via Turbo → success updates button to "Reserved" state
4. Turbo Stream updates customer dashboard reservation count in nav
5. Confirmation email sent via background job

#### Staff Dashboard Flow
1. Admin accesses `/admin/reservations` (protected by admin role)
2. Turbo Frame filters for status/date without page reload
3. Status updates via Turbo Streams broadcast to all admin users
4. Customer notification emails triggered on status changes

---

## Clarifying Questions

Now I need those key details to finalize the implementation:

1. **Reservation Duration**: How long should customers have to pick up reserved books? (3 days? 1 week? 2 weeks?)

2. **Book Inventory Field**: What field tracks available stock on your Book model? (`stock_count`? `quantity`? `inventory`?)

3. **Admin Access**: How do you identify admin users? Is there a `role` field on Customer, or a separate Admin model?

4. **Multiple Copies**: Should customers be limited to one reservation per book title, or can they reserve multiple copies if available?

5. **Pickup Location**: Do you have store address/hours info to display in the reservation modal, or should I include placeholder text for that?

Once you provide these details, I'll deliver the complete specification with all the Rails 7/Hotwire implementation steps, validation rules, and Turbo integration patterns.

# Invoice Management System

## Overview
A Rails application for creating, managing, and tracking invoices with automated status transitions, line item management, and PDF generation capabilities.

## Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Rails 7.1+ with Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS (Rails standard)
- **PDF Generation**: Prawn gem
- **Authentication**: Devise (assumed needed for multi-user invoice management)
- **File Storage**: Active Storage (for PDF storage/caching)

## Data Model

### User
- email: string (required, unique) — authentication
- first_name: string (required) — display name
- last_name: string (required) — display name
- created_at: datetime
- updated_at: datetime
- Relationships: has_many :invoices

### Invoice
- invoice_number: string (required, unique) — auto-generated (e.g., "INV-2024-001")
- status: enum (required) — draft, sent, paid, overdue
- issue_date: date (required) — when invoice was created
- due_date: date (required) — payment deadline
- sent_at: datetime — timestamp when status changed to sent
- paid_at: datetime — timestamp when marked as paid
- subtotal: decimal(10,2) — calculated from line items
- tax_rate: decimal(5,4) (default: 0.0) — percentage as decimal (0.0875 = 8.75%)
- tax_amount: decimal(10,2) — calculated field
- total: decimal(10,2) — subtotal + tax_amount
- notes: text — additional invoice notes
- client_name: string (required) — billing to
- client_email: string (required) — for sending PDFs
- client_address: text — billing address
- user_id: integer (required) — foreign key
- created_at: datetime
- updated_at: datetime
- Relationships: belongs_to :user, has_many :line_items, dependent: :destroy

### LineItem
- description: string (required) — what was provided
- quantity: decimal(8,2) (required, > 0) — amount of units
- unit_price: decimal(10,2) (required, >= 0) — price per unit
- total: decimal(10,2) — calculated (quantity * unit_price)
- invoice_id: integer (required) — foreign key
- created_at: datetime
- updated_at: datetime
- Relationships: belongs_to :invoice

## User Flows

### Create Invoice Flow
1. User clicks "New Invoice" → sees invoice form with empty line items section
2. User fills required fields (client_name, client_email, issue_date, due_date) → real-time validation
3. User adds line items via "Add Line Item" button → new row appears with description, quantity, unit_price fields
4. As user types in line item fields → subtotal/total updates automatically via Stimulus
5. User clicks "Save as Draft" → invoice created with status: draft → redirects to invoice show page with success message
6. Alternative: User clicks "Save and Send" → invoice created with status: sent, sent_at timestamp → PDF generated → redirects to show page

### Edit Invoice Flow
1. User clicks "Edit" on draft invoice → sees pre-populated form
2. User modifies fields and line items → real-time total calculations
3. Existing line items can be removed via "Remove" button with confirmation
4. User saves → invoice updated → redirects to show page
5. Constraint: Only draft invoices can be edited (sent/paid/overdue are read-only)

### Send Invoice Flow
1. User clicks "Send Invoice" on draft invoice → confirmation modal appears
2. Modal shows: "Send invoice [number] to [client_email]? This will generate a PDF and mark it as sent."
3. User confirms → status changes to sent, sent_at timestamp set → PDF generated and stored → success message
4. Background job could email PDF to client (out of scope for MVP)

### Mark as Paid Flow
1. User clicks "Mark as Paid" on sent/overdue invoice → confirmation modal
2. User confirms → status changes to paid, paid_at timestamp set → success message
3. Paid invoices become read-only

### Status Transition (Automatic)
1. Daily background job checks all sent invoices
2. If current_date > due_date and status == sent → status changes to overdue
3. Uses Rails cron job or whenever gem

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **User Authentication**
   - Install and configure Devise
   - User registration/login flows
   - Redirect after login to invoices index

2. **Invoice Model & Basic CRUD**
   - Generate Invoice model with validations
   - Auto-generate invoice_number on create (format: "INV-#{Date.current.year}-#{sequence}")
   - Status enum with valid transitions
   - Basic controller with index, show, new, create, edit, update actions
   - Scoped to current_user (authorization)

3. **Line Items Management**
   - Generate LineItem model with validations
   - Nested attributes on Invoice model (accepts_nested_attributes_for)
   - Dynamic line item form with Stimulus controller for add/remove rows
   - Real-time total calculation via Stimulus

### Phase 2: Core Functionality
4. **Invoice Status Machine**
   - State machine gem (AASM) or custom enum with validation
   - Valid transitions: draft → sent, sent → paid, sent → overdue
   - Prevent editing non-draft invoices
   - Status change actions with timestamp updates

5. **PDF Generation**
   - Prawn gem integration
   - Invoice PDF template with company header, line items table, totals
   - Store generated PDFs with Active Storage
   - Download PDF action

6. **Invoice Interface**
   - Responsive invoice index with status filtering
   - Invoice show page with PDF preview/download
   - Status-specific action buttons (Send, Mark as Paid)
   - Confirmation modals for state changes

### Phase 3: Polish & Automation
7. **Overdue Detection**
   - Background job to mark overdue invoices
   - Rake task for manual execution
   - Status indicators with color coding

8. **Enhanced UX**
   - Toast notifications for actions
   - Loading states on PDF generation
   - Empty states for new users
   - Responsive mobile layout

## Validation Rules

| Field | Rules |
|-------|-------|
| Invoice.client_name | Required, max 255 chars |
| Invoice.client_email | Required, valid email format |
| Invoice.issue_date | Required, cannot be future date |
| Invoice.due_date | Required, must be >= issue_date |
| Invoice.tax_rate | Between 0.0 and 1.0 |
| LineItem.description | Required, max 255 chars |
| LineItem.quantity | Required, > 0, max 2 decimal places |
| LineItem.unit_price | Required, >= 0, max 2 decimal places |
| Invoice status transitions | Only allow draft→sent, sent→paid, sent→overdue |

## Error Handling
- **Form validation errors**: Display inline per field with red styling
- **PDF generation failure**: Show error message, allow retry, log to Rails logger
- **Invalid status transitions**: Prevent with validation, show flash error
- **Concurrent edits**: Use optimistic locking (lock_version) on Invoice model
- **Missing line items**: Require at least one line item before sending

## UI/UX Notes
- **Layout**: Standard Rails layout with sidebar navigation
- **Status indicators**: Color-coded badges (draft: gray, sent: blue, paid: green, overdue: red)
- **Responsive**: Mobile-first design, stack line items vertically on small screens
- **Accessibility**: Proper form labels, focus management in modals, keyboard navigation
- **Loading states**: Disable buttons during PDF generation, show spinner

## Assumptions
- Single-tenant application (each user sees only their invoices)
- USD currency (no multi-currency support)
- Simple tax calculation (single rate applied to subtotal)
- PDF storage in local Active Storage (not external service)
- No email sending in MVP (just PDF generation)
- No recurring invoices or templates
- No payment integration (manual "mark as paid")
- Company/sender details are hardcoded in PDF template
- Invoice numbers are globally unique (not per-user)

## Out of Scope
- Multi-currency support
- Email delivery of invoices
- Payment gateway integration
- Recurring invoice templates
- Advanced reporting/analytics
- Multi-tenant/organization support
- Invoice approval workflows
- Time tracking integration

## Self-Assessment

**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 4/5
- Specificity: 4/5  
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 4/5
- Translation Quality: 4/5

**Issues caught and fixed:**
- Added invoice_number auto-generation logic that wasn't specified
- Included optimistic locking for concurrent edit protection
- Specified exact status transition rules and validation
- Added real-time total calculation via Stimulus (essential for line items UX)
- Included confirmation modals for destructive state changes
- Added background job specification for overdue detection

**Remaining considerations:**
- Company/sender information storage (currently assumed hardcoded in PDF)
- Email delivery mechanism if needed beyond MVP
- Invoice numbering scheme preferences (current format: INV-YYYY-###)

## Questions for Consideration
1. Do you need company/sender information to be configurable per user, or can it be hardcoded in the PDF template?
2. Should invoice numbers be sequential globally or reset per year/user?
3. Do you want email delivery of PDFs to clients, or just PDF generation for manual sending?
4. Any specific PDF styling requirements beyond a clean, professional layout?
5. Should there be any additional invoice statuses (like "cancelled" or "refunded")?

I'll create a draft specification for your Stripe-connected SaaS billing platform, then ask targeted questions about the key ambiguities.

# Stripe-Connected SaaS Billing Platform

## Overview
A SaaS application that allows users to subscribe to monthly plans, connect their own Stripe accounts for payment processing, and manage their billing and subscription lifecycle.

## Tech Stack
- **Backend**: Node.js with Express.js
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Frontend**: React with TypeScript
- **Authentication**: Auth0 or NextAuth.js
- **Payment Processing**: Stripe API (Connect for user accounts)
- **Hosting**: Vercel/Railway for app, AWS RDS for database
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS

## Data Model

### User
- id: UUID (primary key)
- email: string (unique, required)
- name: string (required)
- stripe_customer_id: string (nullable) — Stripe customer ID in platform account
- stripe_account_id: string (nullable) — Connected Stripe account ID
- stripe_account_status: enum (pending, active, restricted, inactive)
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: has_many Subscriptions

### Plan
- id: UUID (primary key)
- name: string (required) — e.g., "Starter", "Pro", "Enterprise"
- description: text
- price_cents: integer (required) — monthly price in cents
- stripe_price_id: string (required) — Stripe Price ID
- features: jsonb — array of feature descriptions
- is_active: boolean (default true)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: has_many Subscriptions

### Subscription
- id: UUID (primary key)
- user_id: UUID (foreign key)
- plan_id: UUID (foreign key)
- stripe_subscription_id: string (required)
- status: enum (active, canceled, past_due, unpaid, incomplete)
- current_period_start: timestamp
- current_period_end: timestamp
- cancel_at_period_end: boolean (default false)
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User, belongs_to Plan

### StripeWebhookEvent
- id: UUID (primary key)
- stripe_event_id: string (unique)
- event_type: string
- processed: boolean (default false)
- created_at: timestamp

## User Flows

### Registration & Onboarding
1. User visits landing page → clicks "Sign Up"
2. User completes registration (email/password) → email verification sent
3. User verifies email → redirected to dashboard with "Connect Stripe" prompt
4. User clicks "Connect Stripe Account" → redirected to Stripe Connect OAuth
5. User authorizes connection → redirected back with success message
6. Dashboard shows available plans for subscription

### Stripe Account Connection
1. User clicks "Connect Stripe Account" → Stripe Connect OAuth flow initiated
2. User logs into Stripe and authorizes the connection
3. System receives authorization code → exchanges for account ID and access token
4. User record updated with stripe_account_id and status set to "pending"
5. System verifies account capabilities → status updated to "active" or "restricted"
6. User sees connection status in dashboard

### Plan Subscription
1. User views available plans on pricing page
2. User clicks "Subscribe" on chosen plan → redirected to checkout
3. Stripe Checkout session created with user's connected account as destination
4. User completes payment → Stripe webhook creates subscription record
5. User redirected to dashboard with active subscription status
6. User gains access to plan features

### Billing Management
1. User navigates to "Billing" section in dashboard
2. User sees current plan, next billing date, payment method
3. User can: update payment method, view invoice history, cancel subscription
4. Cancellation sets cancel_at_period_end=true, shows "Active until [date]"
5. Plan changes create new subscription, cancel old one at period end

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **User Authentication System**
   - Registration with email verification
   - Login/logout with session management
   - Password reset flow
   - Protected routes middleware
   - User dashboard layout with navigation

2. **Plan Management (Admin)**
   - Create/edit/deactivate subscription plans
   - Set pricing and feature lists
   - Sync with Stripe Price objects
   - Admin interface for plan management

### Phase 2: Core Billing Functionality
3. **Stripe Connect Integration**
   - OAuth flow for connecting user Stripe accounts
   - Account verification and capability checking
   - Connection status display and management
   - Disconnect/reconnect functionality

4. **Subscription Management**
   - Plan selection and checkout flow
   - Stripe Checkout integration with Connect accounts
   - Subscription status tracking and display
   - Plan upgrade/downgrade functionality

5. **Webhook Processing**
   - Stripe webhook endpoint with signature verification
   - Event processing for subscription lifecycle events
   - Idempotent event handling with deduplication
   - Error handling and retry logic for failed webhooks

### Phase 3: Billing Management & Polish
6. **Customer Billing Portal**
   - View current subscription and billing history
   - Update payment methods
   - Download invoices
   - Cancel subscription with confirmation

7. **Admin Dashboard**
   - View all users and subscription statuses
   - Revenue analytics and reporting
   - Failed payment management
   - Stripe account connection monitoring

## Validation Rules
- Email: valid format, unique across users
- Plan price: positive integer, required
- Stripe IDs: format validation when present
- Subscription status: must be valid enum value
- Password: minimum 8 characters, at least one number and special character

## Error Handling
- Stripe API failures: retry with exponential backoff, log for manual review
- Webhook failures: return 200 to Stripe but log error, implement retry queue
- Payment failures: email user, show in dashboard, provide retry mechanism
- Connection failures: clear error messages, retry options
- Form validation: inline field-level errors with specific messaging

## UI/UX Notes
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach
- Loading states for all Stripe API calls and redirects
- Success/error toast notifications for all actions
- Stripe-branded elements for payment flows
- Clear subscription status indicators (active, canceled, past due)
- Accessible form labels and keyboard navigation

## Assumptions
- Users have their own Stripe accounts (not creating accounts for them)
- Monthly billing only (no annual or custom intervals)
- Single subscription per user (no multiple active subscriptions)
- USD currency only
- Platform takes a percentage fee through Stripe Connect
- Users manage their own customer support (platform doesn't handle end-customer issues)
- No free trial period (immediate payment required)
- No usage-based billing (flat monthly rates only)

## Out of Scope
- Multi-currency support
- Annual billing cycles
- Usage-based or metered billing
- Customer support ticketing system
- Advanced analytics beyond basic revenue reporting
- API access for third-party integrations
- White-label or multi-tenant features

---

## Key Questions

Before I finalize this specification, I need clarification on these critical aspects:

1. **Business Model**: Are you taking a platform fee from transactions (typical 2-5% on top of Stripe's fees), or is this a flat SaaS fee where users pay you directly for the service?

2. **User's Customers**: Who are your users billing? Are they selling products, services, or subscriptions to their own customers? This affects what Stripe products they need connected.

3. **Plan Structure**: Should there be multiple tiers with different features (like usage limits, API access, etc.), or is this more about different price points for the same core service?

4. **Stripe Account Requirements**: Do users need fully verified Stripe accounts, or can they start with restricted accounts? This affects the onboarding flow complexity.

5. **Payment Flow**: When a user's customer pays them, does that payment go directly to the user's Stripe account, or does it flow through your platform account first?

These answers will significantly impact the data model, user flows, and Stripe integration approach.

# Stripe-Connected SaaS Billing Platform

## Overview
A SaaS application that allows users to subscribe to tiered monthly plans (Free, Pro, Enterprise), connect their own Stripe accounts for payment processing, and manage their billing and subscription lifecycle with project limits and team features.

## Tech Stack
- **Backend**: Node.js with Express.js
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Frontend**: React with TypeScript
- **Authentication**: Auth0 or NextAuth.js
- **Payment Processing**: Stripe API (Connect for user accounts)
- **Email**: SendGrid or AWS SES for receipts and notifications
- **Hosting**: Vercel/Railway for app, AWS RDS for database
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS

## Data Model

### User
- id: UUID (primary key)
- email: string (unique, required)
- name: string (required)
- stripe_customer_id: string (nullable) — Stripe customer ID in platform account
- stripe_account_id: string (nullable) — Connected Stripe account ID
- stripe_account_status: enum (pending, active, restricted, inactive)
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: has_many Subscriptions, has_many Projects, has_many TeamMemberships

### Plan
- id: UUID (primary key)
- name: string (required) — "Free", "Pro", "Enterprise"
- description: text
- price_cents: integer (required) — 0, 2900, 9900
- stripe_price_id: string (nullable) — null for free plan
- project_limit: integer (nullable) — 3 for free, null for unlimited
- team_features_enabled: boolean (default false)
- is_active: boolean (default true)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: has_many Subscriptions

### Subscription
- id: UUID (primary key)
- user_id: UUID (foreign key)
- plan_id: UUID (foreign key)
- stripe_subscription_id: string (nullable) — null for free plan
- status: enum (active, canceled, past_due, unpaid, incomplete, trialing)
- current_period_start: timestamp
- current_period_end: timestamp
- cancel_at_period_end: boolean (default false)
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User, belongs_to Plan

### Project
- id: UUID (primary key)
- user_id: UUID (foreign key)
- name: string (required)
- description: text
- created_at: timestamp
- updated_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User

### TeamMembership (Enterprise only)
- id: UUID (primary key)
- user_id: UUID (foreign key) — team owner
- member_email: string (required)
- role: enum (member, admin)
- status: enum (pending, active, inactive)
- invited_at: timestamp
- joined_at: timestamp (nullable)
- Relationships: belongs_to User

### StripeWebhookEvent
- id: UUID (primary key)
- stripe_event_id: string (unique)
- event_type: string
- processed: boolean (default false)
- created_at: timestamp

## User Flows

### Registration & Free Plan Setup
1. User visits landing page → clicks "Sign Up"
2. User completes registration (email/password) → email verification sent
3. User verifies email → automatically assigned to Free plan
4. User redirected to dashboard showing "3 projects remaining" and upgrade prompts
5. User can create up to 3 projects immediately without payment

### Plan Upgrade (Free → Pro/Enterprise)
1. User clicks "Upgrade" button or hits project limit
2. User selects Pro ($29/mo) or Enterprise ($99/mo) plan
3. Stripe Checkout session created for platform account (not user's connected account)
4. User enters credit card details → completes payment
5. Webhook processes successful payment → subscription created, plan upgraded
6. User redirected to dashboard with new plan features unlocked
7. Email receipt sent automatically

### Plan Change (Pro ↔ Enterprise)
1. User navigates to "Billing" → clicks "Change Plan"
2. User selects new plan → sees prorated amount preview
3. User confirms change → Stripe processes proration immediately
4. Old subscription canceled, new subscription created
5. User gains/loses features immediately based on new plan
6. Email receipt sent for prorated charge/credit

### Downgrade to Free
1. User clicks "Downgrade to Free" in billing section
2. Warning shown: "You'll lose access to unlimited projects and team features at the end of your billing period"
3. User confirms → subscription set to cancel at period end
4. User retains paid features until period expires
5. At period end: subscription canceled, plan downgraded to Free, projects over limit archived

### Stripe Account Connection (Optional)
1. User clicks "Connect Stripe Account" in settings
2. Stripe Connect OAuth flow initiated
3. User authorizes connection → account ID stored
4. Connection status displayed in dashboard
5. User can disconnect/reconnect anytime

### Team Management (Enterprise Only)
1. Enterprise user navigates to "Team" section
2. User invites members by email → invitation emails sent
3. Invited users create accounts or log in → join team automatically
4. Team owner can manage member roles and remove members
5. Team members see shared projects and features

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **User Authentication & Free Plan**
   - Registration with email verification
   - Login/logout with session management
   - Password reset flow
   - Automatic Free plan assignment on registration
   - User dashboard with plan status and project count
   - Protected routes with plan-based feature gating

2. **Project Management**
   - Create/edit/delete projects (respecting plan limits)
   - Project list with search and filtering
   - Plan limit enforcement: disable "Create Project" when limit reached
   - Archive projects when downgrading (don't delete, just hide)
   - Project limit indicator in UI: "2 of 3 projects used"

### Phase 2: Billing & Subscriptions
3. **Plan Management System**
   - Seed database with Free/Pro/Enterprise plans
   - Plan comparison page with feature matrix
   - Upgrade prompts throughout UI when hitting limits
   - Plan change preview with prorated amounts

4. **Stripe Subscription Integration**
   - Stripe Checkout for plan upgrades
   - Subscription creation and management
   - Proration handling for plan changes
   - Automatic plan downgrades at period end
   - Payment method updates through Stripe Customer Portal

5. **Webhook Processing & Email Receipts**
   - Stripe webhook endpoint with signature verification
   - Process subscription lifecycle events (created, updated, canceled, payment_succeeded, payment_failed)
   - Send email receipts after successful charges
   - Handle failed payments with email notifications
   - Idempotent event processing with deduplication

### Phase 3: Advanced Features
6. **Team Features (Enterprise)**
   - Team member invitation system
   - Role-based permissions (member vs admin)
   - Shared project access for team members
   - Team member management interface
   - Team-only features and UI sections

7. **Billing Management Portal**
   - Current subscription details and next billing date
   - Plan change interface with prorated previews
   - Payment method management (redirect to Stripe Customer Portal)
   - Invoice history and downloads
   - Cancellation flow with retention offers

8. **Optional Stripe Connect Integration**
   - OAuth flow for connecting user Stripe accounts
   - Connection status display and management
   - Account verification status
   - Disconnect functionality

## Validation Rules
- Email: valid format, unique across users
- Project name: required, max 100 characters
- Plan limits: enforce project count based on current plan
- Team invitations: valid email format, not already a team member
- Subscription changes: validate plan transitions and proration calculations

## Error Handling
- **Stripe API failures**: Retry with exponential backoff, show user-friendly error messages
- **Webhook failures**: Return 200 to Stripe, log errors, implement retry queue for critical events
- **Payment failures**: Email user immediately, show payment retry options in dashboard
- **Plan limit violations**: Clear messaging when limits reached, prominent upgrade CTAs
- **Team invitation failures**: Specific error messages for duplicate invites, invalid emails
- **Email delivery failures**: Log for manual follow-up, don't block user flows

## Interaction Feedback
- **Plan upgrades**: Show loading spinner during Stripe redirect, success toast on return
- **Project creation**: Disable form and show "Creating..." while saving
- **Plan changes**: Show prorated amount calculation in real-time
- **Team invitations**: Show "Invitation sent" confirmation, update member list immediately
- **Payment method updates**: Redirect to Stripe portal with loading state
- **Subscription cancellation**: Confirmation modal with clear consequences

## Edge Cases
- **Project limit enforcement**: Archive excess projects when downgrading, restore when upgrading
- **Failed payments**: Grace period before feature restrictions, multiple retry attempts
- **Concurrent plan changes**: Lock subscription during changes to prevent race conditions
- **Team member limits**: Prevent inviting more members than Enterprise plan allows
- **Webhook replay attacks**: Verify webhook signatures and deduplicate events
- **Partial refunds**: Handle prorated credits correctly when downgrading mid-cycle

## State Preservation
- **Plan comparison page**: Maintain scroll position when returning from Stripe Checkout
- **Project list**: Preserve filters and pagination across navigation
- **Billing page**: Show real-time subscription status updates
- **Team management**: Optimistic UI updates for invitations, rollback on failure

## UI/UX Notes
- **Responsive design**: Mobile-first approach with touch-friendly buttons
- **Plan indicators**: Clear badges showing current plan throughout the app
- **Upgrade prompts**: Contextual CTAs when users hit plan limits
- **Loading states**: Skeleton screens for project lists, spinners for form submissions
- **Empty states**: Helpful messaging for new users with no projects
- **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
- **Plan comparison**: Clear feature matrix with visual hierarchy

## Assumptions
- **Payment processing**: Platform charges users directly (not through their Stripe accounts)
- **Currency**: USD only for initial launch
- **Billing cycle**: Monthly only, no annual discounts
- **Team size**: No hard limit on Enterprise team members
- **Project archiving**: Archived projects don't count toward limits but remain accessible
- **Email receipts**: Sent immediately after successful payment
- **Free plan**: No time limit, permanent free tier
- **Stripe Connect**: Optional feature for users who want to accept payments

## Out of Scope
- **Multi-currency support**
- **Annual billing cycles or discounts**
- **Usage-based billing or metered features**
- **Advanced team permissions beyond member/admin**
- **Project templates or advanced project features**
- **API access or webhooks for third parties**
- **White-label or reseller features**
- **Advanced analytics beyond basic subscription metrics**

## Self-Assessment
**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 5/5
- Specificity: 5/5  
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 4/5
- Translation Quality: 5/5
- Implementation Coverage: 5/5

**Issues caught and fixed:**
- Added Project entity and relationship modeling for plan limits
- Specified proration handling for plan changes with immediate feature access
- Added team membership system for Enterprise features
- Clarified that platform charges users directly (not through connected Stripe accounts)
- Added project archiving behavior for downgrades
- Included comprehensive interaction feedback patterns for all user actions
- Added email receipt automation after successful payments

**Remaining considerations:**
- Consider adding usage analytics to help users understand their project activity
- May want to add plan usage warnings before hitting limits (e.g., "2 of 3 projects used")
- Could implement retention offers during cancellation flow to reduce churn

The specification is now complete and ready for implementation. Each phase builds logically on the previous one, and all user interactions have defined feedback patterns and error handling.
